Record Class ExecutionSnapshot

java.lang.Object
java.lang.Record
dk.tij.registermaschine.core.runtime.ExecutionSnapshot
Record Components:
programmeCounter - the current programme counter value
registers - a map containing only registers that changed since the previous snapshot, indexed by register number
negative - the current state of the negative flag
zero - the current state of the zero flag
overflow - the current state of the overflow flag
exitCode - the program exit code if execution has terminated
output - the most recent output value produced by the program, or null if no output occurred since the last snapshot
public record ExecutionSnapshot(int programmeCounter, Map<Integer,Integer> registers, boolean negative, boolean zero, boolean overflow, byte exitCode, Integer output) extends Record
Immutable snapshot of an IExecutionContext at a specific moment in time.

An ExecutionSnapshot captures the relevant runtime state changes of the Registermaschine so that external systems such as user interfaces, debugging or monitoring tools can inspect the machine state without directly interacting with the live execution context.

The snapshot contains the current programme counter, machine status flags, exit code, optional output value, and the set of registers that have changed since the previous snapshot.

The registers map does not necessarily contain all registers. Instead, it contains only the registers whose values were modified since the last snapshot. Observers are expected to maintain their own register state and apply these changes incrementally.

Snapshots are usually created by IExecutionContext.snapshotAndClearDirty() which also clears the internal "dirty" tracking after the snapshot is taken.

Because this type is implemented as a record, all fields are immutable once the snapshot has been created.

Since:
1.0.0
Author:
TiJ

  • Constructor Details

    • ExecutionSnapshot

      public ExecutionSnapshot(int programmeCounter, Map<Integer,Integer> registers, boolean negative, boolean zero, boolean overflow, byte exitCode, Integer output)
      Creates an instance of a ExecutionSnapshot record class.
      Parameters:
      programmeCounter - the value for the programmeCounter record component
      registers - the value for the registers record component
      negative - the value for the negative record component
      zero - the value for the zero record component
      overflow - the value for the overflow record component
      exitCode - the value for the exitCode record component
      output - the value for the output record component

  • Method Details

    • toString

      public final String toString()
      Returns a string representation of this record class. The representation contains the name of the class, followed by the name and value of each of the record components.
      Specified by:
      toString in class Record
      Returns:
      a string representation of this object

    • hashCode

      public final int hashCode()
      Returns a hash code value for this object. The value is derived from the hash code of each of the record components.
      Specified by:
      hashCode in class Record
      Returns:
      a hash code value for this object

    • equals

      public final boolean equals(Object o)
      Indicates whether some other object is "equal to" this one. The objects are equal if the other object is of the same class and if all the record components are equal. Reference components are compared with Objects::equals(Object,Object); primitive components are compared with the compare method from their corresponding wrapper classes.
      Specified by:
      equals in class Record
      Parameters:
      o - the object with which to compare
      Returns:
      true if this object is the same as the o argument; false otherwise.
